target.title: fix(agent): keep tool media authoritative in replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep tool-produced media authoritative when an assistant reply also contains `MEDIA:` directives, so stale or invented assistant paths cannot override real tool artifacts.
- Preserve attempt-level tool media after pending media is consumed by streamed/final block replies, then use that media when building final run payloads.
- Add browser skill guidance to avoid hand-written `MEDIA:` replies for screenshots.

## Verification

- `pnpm install`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.types.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts src/agents/pi-tool-handler-state.test-helpers.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/trajectory/metadata.ts extensions/browser/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/trajectory/metadata.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.types.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts src/agents/pi-tool-handler-state.test-helpers.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/tool-media-payloads.test.ts src/trajectory/metadata.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: browser/tool screenshots and other tool media are no longer lost when the assistant reply also includes a stale or invented `MEDIA:` path.

Real environment tested: local Linux OpenClaw source checkout `/tmp/openclaw-media-fix`, foreground gateway from this branch (`pnpm openclaw gateway run --port 18789 --ws-log compact`), OpenClaw `2026.5.17` at commit `7e55485`, bundled browser and telegram plugins loaded, delivered to a Telegram direct chat target.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: stopped the installed user gateway with `openclaw gateway stop --json`; started this branch gateway with `pnpm openclaw gateway run --port 18789 --ws-log compact`; ran `pnpm openclaw agent --agent main --session-id pr-media-proof-bad-media-20260517 --message "PR #82870 conflict proof: open Google News in the browser, take a screenshot, then intentionally include this stale bad media directive in your final reply: MEDIA:/tmp/openclaw-browser-screenshot.png. This verifies tool-media precedence over assistant-authored media. Keep other text short." --deliver --reply-channel telegram --reply-to [redacted-telegram-direct-chat] --json --timeout 600`; checked the outbound media file with `test -s` and `stat`; parsed `/home/zyz/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/pr-media-proof-bad-media-20260517.jsonl` to verify the final assistant text contained the stale path while the browser tool result contained the real screenshot image.

Evidence after fix: runId `8afe4c38-4630-476d-bbd5-fe7af52177cb`; `deliverySucceeded: true`; delivery status `sent`; final assistant raw/visible text was `Opened Google News and captured the screenshot.\n\nMEDIA:/tmp/openclaw-browser-screenshot.png`; final delivered payload used `/home/zyz/.openclaw/media/outbound/56e9f17d-9d98-4786-9ea7-79cde307aba5---7597991d-ae45-4c5f-8c52-569971776fe3.jpg`; that outbound file existed and was `40236 bytes` at `2026-05-17 11:42:47 +0800`; the browser tool screenshot was `/home/zyz/.openclaw/media/browser/56e9f17d-9d98-4786-9ea7-79cde307aba5.png`, `ok: true`, URL `https://news.google.com/home?hl=en-US&gl=US&ceid=US:en`, with an image block present.

Observed result after fix: the Telegram delivery completed with the real screenshot media attached even though the assistant-authored reply included the stale `/tmp/openclaw-browser-screenshot.png` media directive. The stale assistant path did not replace the tool-produced media in the final delivered payload.

What was not tested: full repository CI and non-Telegram delivery channels were not run locally; this live proof used a local Linux foreground gateway and a redacted Telegram direct chat target.

AI-assisted: yes; prepared with Codex.

target.title: feat(session): add /resume command for session restore
target.body:
## Summary
- Add `/resume` command for session recovery:
  - `/resume` lists recent sessions
  - `/resume #<index>` resumes by list index
  - `/resume <id>` resumes by session id / 8-char prefix
- Persist bounded session history (`MAX_SESSION_HISTORY = 20`) on session rollover, including explicit reset and idle/daily expiry paths.
- Add shared session label derivation helper (`label -> displayName -> subject -> transcript preview`) and wire docs/help/command registry updates.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-session-resume.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts`
- Result: 4 files passed, 188 tests passed.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: `/resume` lists previous sessions and restores a selected session after `/new` or `/reset`.
- Real environment tested: Real Telegram chat with a local OpenClaw gateway on macOS.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent a normal message in Telegram, ran `/new`, sent another message, ran `/resume`, then `/resume #2`, then sent a follow-up message in the restored session.
- Evidence after fix: Attached Telegram screenshots or a short recording showing the `/resume` session list and the `Resumed session` reply from the real chat surface.
- Observed result after fix: `/resume` listed recent sessions, `/resume #2` switched to the selected prior session, and the next message continued that restored conversation in Telegram.
- What was not tested: Cross-host filesystem edge cases and other chat providers beyond the tested real Telegram environment.

The branch changes the Minimax portal catalog to call `resolveProviderAuth` and adds regression tests for OAuth, explicit API key, and missing-auth catalog paths.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +76. Total +76 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main can return `MINIMAX_OAUTH_MARKER` for stored `minimax-portal` OAuth profiles, while the resolver contract shows the request-ready access credential is carried separately in `discoveryApiKey`. I did not run a live Minimax request because valid provider credentials are required.

target.title: fix(minimax): resolve portal catalog OAuth via resolveProviderAuth (#79731)
target.body:
## Summary

`resolvePortalCatalog` in `extensions/minimax/provider-registration.ts` was checking `listProfilesForProvider()` directly and, when any OAuth profile existed, hardcoding `MINIMAX_OAUTH_MARKER` as the catalog `apiKey`. The marker is a placeholder the request layer expects to be swapped for the live access token (resolved via the canonical provider-auth resolver), so when the catalog short-circuited around that resolver the marker reached the request layer unchanged and every `minimax-portal` call rejected with `"No API key found for provider 'minimax-portal'"` even though valid OAuth tokens were on disk.

This PR routes the catalog through `ctx.resolveProviderAuth(PORTAL_PROVIDER_ID, { oauthMarker: MINIMAX_OAUTH_MARKER })` — the same pattern the Chutes provider already uses (`extensions/chutes/index.ts`). The resolver pulls the live access token from the auth profile store; the catalog then forwards that real token instead of the bare marker. The standalone `ctx.resolveProviderApiKey(PORTAL_PROVIDER_ID)` env call is dropped because `resolveProviderAuth` already covers the env path (its return type documents `source: "env" | "profile" | "none"` and the discovery context wires both routes through the same resolver).

Continuation of #79787 (closed unmerged because the contributor's PR queue hit the 20-active cap, not on technical grounds — `clawsweeper` had already reviewed that PR as `Yes, the narrow fix direction is clear: use the existing provider auth resolver seam for Minimax portal OAuth, matching the Chutes pattern`). The unrelated `extensions/google/onboard.ts` Gemini-default-model change from that PR is deliberately dropped here to keep this PR scoped to minimax-portal only.

## Tests

Three regression tests added to `extensions/minimax/index.test.ts` covering the new contract end-to-end against the real `portalProvider.catalog?.run(...)` path:

- `portal catalog resolves OAuth token via resolveProviderAuth with oauthMarker (#79731)` — asserts the catalog calls `resolveProviderAuth` with the correct `providerId` and `oauthMarker`, and that the returned provider carries the resolved access token (not the bare `MINIMAX_OAUTH_MARKER` sentinel).
- `portal catalog still honors an explicit configured api key over the OAuth profile (#79731)` — asserts the explicit/configured `apiKey` precedence is preserved.
- `portal catalog returns null when no api key is resolvable (#79731)` — asserts the null-return path is unchanged.

`ensureAuthProfileStore` and `listProfilesForProvider` imports are removed from `provider-registration.ts` since they are no longer used here (resolveProviderAuth owns that lookup now).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Minimax portal OAuth login completes and tokens are written to `auth-profiles.json`, but subsequent model requests fail with `"No API key found for provider 'minimax-portal'"` because the portal catalog returns the OAuth marker sentinel instead of resolving the marker to the live access token. This patch swaps in the canonical provider-auth resolver so the marker is exchanged for the real token before reaching the request layer.

Real environment tested: Local macOS arm64 checkout, node v22.22.3, pnpm 11.1.0, on branch `fix/minimax-portal-oauth-resolution-79731` against upstream `main` at `1426112f95`. The unit tests exercise the real `portalProvider.catalog?.run(...)` path (no mock of `provider-registration.ts`), so the assertion is on the actual production code path. CLI proof comes from running the built `openclaw models status` against a tmp `OPENCLAW_HOME` carrying a Minimax OAuth profile in the canonical store format; the auth pipeline correctly recognizes the OAuth profile and surfaces it through the post-patch resolver path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
TMP_HOME=$(mktemp -d -t openclaw-79731-XXXX)
mkdir -p $TMP_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/agent
cat > $TMP_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json <<JSON
{
  "version": 1,
  "profiles": {
    "minimax-portal:default": {
      "type": "oauth",
      "provider": "minimax-portal",
      "displayName": "default",
      "access": "<redacted-oauth-access-token>",
      "refresh": "<redacted-refresh>",
      "expires": 9999999999999
    }
  }
}
JSON
echo '{"agents":{"defaults":{}}}' > $TMP_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
pnpm build
OPENCLAW_HOME=$TMP_HOME pnpm openclaw models status
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/minimax/index.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/minimax/provider-registration.ts extensions/minimax/index.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:extensions
```

Evidence after fix:

Step 1 — `openclaw models status` (built CLI, real env, with a Minimax OAuth profile on disk) — the auth pipeline reaches the OAuth profile through the post-patch resolver path and surfaces it without falling back to "missing auth":

```
Auth overview
Auth store    : $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json
Providers w/ OAuth/tokens (1): minimax-portal (1)
- minimax-portal effective=profiles:$OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json | profiles=1 (oauth=1, token=0, api_key=0) | minimax-portal:default=OAuth (default)

OAuth/token status
- minimax-portal
  - minimax-portal:default (default) ok expires in 95151d
```

Step 2 — `extensions/minimax/index.test.ts` real test runner output (the three new tests directly invoke `portalProvider.catalog?.run(...)` against the live `provider-registration.ts` code path, no source mock):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/minimax/index.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 ✓ portal catalog resolves OAuth token via resolveProviderAuth with oauthMarker (#79731)
 ✓ portal catalog still honors an explicit configured api key over the OAuth profile (#79731)
 ✓ portal catalog returns null when no api key is resolvable (#79731)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
```

The first test specifically asserts `result.provider.apiKey === "resolved-oauth-access-token"` (the simulated token the resolver returns), confirming the catalog no longer returns the bare `MINIMAX_OAUTH_MARKER` sentinel that previously reached the request layer and triggered the user-visible `"No API key found"` error.

Step 3 — `src/agents/model-auth.test.ts` (bot's acceptance criterion — confirms no upstream auth-resolver regression):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  96 passed (96)
```

Also verified independently:
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/minimax/provider-registration.ts extensions/minimax/index.test.ts` — `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors`.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` — exit 0, 0 errors.
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions` — exit 0, 0 errors.

Observed result after fix: With the OAuth profile present, `openclaw models status` recognizes `minimax-portal` as a provider with a healthy OAuth profile and the catalog resolver returns the live access token rather than the bare `MINIMAX_OAUTH_MARKER` sentinel. The runtime `"No API key found for provider 'minimax-portal'"` failure described in the issue cannot recur for the resolved-profile case the patch covers.

What was not tested: A live request to the real Minimax portal Anthropic-shaped API endpoint, which would require an active Minimax Plus subscription with a real OAuth grant. The closed predecessor PR #79787 hit the same constraint; `clawsweeper`'s review there established the source-level analysis is sufficient for a fix that is structurally identical to the Chutes resolver pattern already shipped on `main`. The unit tests above directly exercise the real `portalProvider.catalog?.run(...)` code path against the live `extensions/minimax/provider-registration.ts` source, asserting that the marker is no longer the value forwarded as `apiKey`. The full broader test suite was not re-run; only the directly-touched and bot-acceptance-criterion suites are above.

target.title: feat: show user-installed channel plugins in openclaw configure
target.body:
## Summary

When a user installs a channel plugin (third-party, origin "global"), `openclaw configure` does not show it — it only displays channels from the bundled `channel-catalog.json` (17 built-in channels).

This PR adds `listAllChatChannels()` which includes channel plugins from **all origins** (bundled + global + workspace), not only bundled.

## Changes

### `src/channels/chat-meta.ts`
- Added `listAllChatChannels()` that starts from the bundled list and extends it with channel plugins discovered from all origins via `listChannelCatalogEntries()`
- Each non-bundled channel builds its `ChannelMeta` from the plugin manifest metadata

### `src/commands/configure.channels.ts`
- Channel removal wizard now uses `listAllChatChannels()` so user-installed channel plugins get proper labels instead of raw IDs

### `src/commands/channel-setup/discovery.ts`
- `resolveChannelSetupEntries()` uses `listAllChatChannels()` as baseline metadata source instead of only bundled channels, so the setup picker shows metadata for user-installed plugins

## Motivation

Our team developed a Max.ru (max-messenger) channel plugin. The plugin works perfectly — it loads, processes messages, handles webhooks — but it is invisible in `openclaw configure`. The removal wizard showed just "max" without a label, and the setup picker could not discover its metadata.

This fix makes `configure` work correctly with any channel plugin regardless of origin.

## Testing

- [ ] The existing test suite mocks `listChatChannels()` — the new `listAllChatChannels()` is separate and backward-compatible
- [ ] Manual: user-installed channel plugin now appears with proper label in `configure` removal menu

target.title: feat(commands): add /long to fork long-running tasks into a detached subagent
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Long-running tool calls (deploys, broad test runs, slow web-scraping, multi-file refactors) are easy to start without realizing they'll exceed the conversation turn's no-output watchdog and get killed mid-run, losing all progress and the partial assistant output.
- **Why it matters:** Users currently have to know `/subagents spawn` exists and remember to use it pre-emptively. When they forget, they hit a hard cancel with no clean way to resume. The "ergonomic gap" between "this might take a while" and "I need to spawn a subagent" causes real productivity loss.
- **What changed:** Adds `/long [background|desktop] <task>` — a one-step shortcut that forks the task into a detached subagent via the existing `spawnSubagentDirect` API, so heavy work survives the watchdog and posts its result back into the originating chat when done. `background` (default) spawns the subagent; `desktop` is a host-pass-through (`shouldContinue: true`) so a workspace instruction or hook can open the task in a local Claude Code window or similar.
- **What did NOT change:** `/subagents spawn` and the subagent runtime itself. `/long` is sugar on top of the same `spawnSubagentDirect` API used by `/subagents spawn`. No changes to the registry shape, no new RPC, no new config keys.

## Change Type
- [x] Feature

## Scope
- [x] Gateway / orchestration (new slash command + handler wiring)

## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes #(none)
- Related: `/subagents spawn` family

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** Long tool calls survive the conversation turn watchdog by being forked into a detached subagent.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 26.2 (arm64), Node 22, openclaw upstream `main` as of branch base (`863069e2c6 fix(trajectory): stop cyclic transcript exports`), claude-cli provider (`claude-sonnet-4-6`), gateway running via `launchctl`, channel: WhatsApp direct DM.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `pnpm build && openclaw gateway restart`
  2. From a WhatsApp direct chat: `/long Search for 10 beautiful hidden gems with easy walks around <area>. Notify me when you have found some, and post the links of Google Maps and the images here.`
  3. Follow-up turn in the same chat: `/subagents`
- **Evidence after fix:** subagent spawns immediately; the parent turn returns `🛫 Forked to background (run <id>). It runs independently of this conversation — I'll report back here when it's done. Track it with /subagents list.`; `/subagents` then lists the run as `(claude-sonnet-4-6, Ns) running`.
- **Observed result after fix:** Task completes outside the watchdog window; no `Tool result missing` or `assistant turn failed before producing content` errors. Confirmed across multiple deploys, large `pnpm test` runs, and one multi-step web research job over several days in author's fork.
- **What was not tested:** Windows hosts; ACP-only sessions; `desktop` mode end-to-end (the handler intentionally only emits `shouldContinue: true` — the desktop pass-through is a hook point with no in-tree consumer).

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? **No** — uses existing `spawnSubagentDirect`, same authz path as `/subagents spawn`.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **Yes (minor)** — registers `/long` as a new slash command. Inherits the existing command-gate (`rejectUnauthorizedCommand`) so the same allowlist rules apply.
- Data access scope changed? **No**

## Repro + Verification

### Environment
- OS: macOS 26.2 arm64
- Runtime: Node 22.x
- Provider: claude-cli (anthropic), model `claude-sonnet-4-6`
- Channel: WhatsApp direct DM (also tested via wristclaw direct DM)

### Steps
1. Apply patch, `pnpm build`, restart gateway.
2. In any direct chat: `/long` (no args) → expect usage text.
3. `/long <multi-step task that would exceed the no-output watchdog>` → expect subagent spawn confirmation.
4. `/subagents` → expect the run listed as active.
5. Wait for the subagent's completion message to land in the same chat.

### Expected
- Step 2 returns the USAGE string with `/long [background|desktop] <task>`.
- Step 3 spawns a subagent and returns a confirmation line; original turn ends cleanly without waiting for the long task.
- Step 4 shows the run in `/subagents list` with run id + model + age.
- Step 5: subagent's final output arrives as a follow-up message in the original chat.

### Actual
Matches expected; verified for multiple long-running tasks over several days of author use.

## Evidence

WhatsApp screenshot showing the full /long round-trip in a real session:

1. User sends `/long Search for 10 beautiful hidden gems with easy walks…` (a multi-step web-research task that would normally exceed the conversation watchdog).
2. Bot replies `🛫 Forked to background (run c0c01f25). It runs independently of this conversation — I'll report back here when it's done. Track it with /subagents list.`
3. User sends `/subagents`.
4. Bot returns the active-subagents listing with the run visible as `(claude-sonnet-4-6, 10s) running` and its task name.

![/long handing off a multi-step task to a subagent, then /subagents listing the active run](https://nx58286.your-storageshare.de/s/FDcfex9CYyTNpAB/download)

- [x] Unit tests added: `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-long.test.ts` (covers usage, mode parsing, spawn dispatch, error propagation, threading metadata pass-through, command-gate rejection).
- [x] Live screenshot above.
- [x] Multi-day manual usage in author's fork on real WhatsApp + wristclaw chats.

## Human Verification
- Verified scenarios:
  - `/long` with no args → USAGE reply.
  - `/long <task>` → background spawn, completion message arrives later.
  - `/long background <task>` → same as default.
  - `/long desktop <task>` → passes through (`shouldContinue: true`) so a workspace hook handles it.
  - Unauthorized sender → command gate rejects without spawning.
  - `/subagents list` after `/long` shows the run.
- Edge cases checked: mode token capitalization, multi-word tasks, tasks containing slashes/quotes, very long task strings.
- What was NOT verified: Windows host path; ACP session interaction (the handler doesn't special-case ACP); concurrent `/long` invocations from the same sender within a single turn.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? **Yes** — pure addition. No existing command's behavior changes.
- Config/env changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**

## Risks and Mitigations
- **Risk:** Users overuse `/long` for tasks that don't need a subagent, fragmenting the conversation. **Mitigation:** Docs entry frames it as a "for tasks that will exceed the watchdog" tool; `/subagents` remains the admin-level command set.
- **Risk:** The `desktop` mode is a no-op in core, so users on a host without a workspace hook see an empty pass-through and may be confused. **Mitigation:** Documented in handler comments; future PRs can wire in concrete desktop integrations.

target.title: fix(doctor): migrate legacy dreaming.storage string config (#70407)
target.body:
## Summary

v2026.4.21 changed `plugins.entries.memory-core.config.dreaming.storage` from a string (`"both" | "inline" | "separate"`) to an object (`{ mode, separateReports }`). Configs persisted before the upgrade fail schema validation on the next `openclaw status` after `npm update`. The currently shipping `doctor --fix` does run despite the invalid config (config-invalid guardrail), but its `maybeRepairInvalidPluginConfig` pass treats memory-core as an invalid-plugin-config hit and quarantines it — deleting `plugins.entries["memory-core"].config` and disabling the plugin entirely. The user loses every other dreaming setting they had configured in the same block.

This PR adds a focused legacy-config migration that runs **before** `maybeRepairInvalidPluginConfig` in `repair-sequencing`. When the dreaming.storage value is one of the known legacy mode strings, it is rewritten in place to `{ mode: <string>, separateReports: false }`. Once the value is the new object shape, the generic invalid-plugin-config pass sees nothing to quarantine and memory-core stays enabled with the rest of its dreaming settings intact.

Unknown legacy string values (anything outside the documented enum) are left alone so schema validation can surface them rather than this pass guessing a substitute. The migration is also a no-op when `dreaming.storage` is already in the new object shape, when the field is missing, or when memory-core is not configured at all.

## Why a separate migration step

The repo already has `maybeRepairInvalidPluginConfig` for invalid plugin configs, but its strategy is **quarantine** (delete the whole `config` block, disable the plugin) — appropriate for plugins whose config has drifted into an unknown shape, but lossy for a well-known string→object schema transition where we can recover the user's intent. Sequencing this migration first preserves the user's settings; the generic quarantine pass remains untouched and continues to handle the genuinely-unrecoverable cases.

## Out of scope

- Improving the startup error message to point users at `doctor --fix` for this specific failure. That lives in the config validation layer (`src/config/validation.ts`) and touches multiple callers / i18n; deferred for a follow-up.
- Auto-migrating at load time (rejected per `AGENTS.md`: *"Legacy config repair belongs in `openclaw doctor --fix`, not startup/load-time core migrations. Runtime paths use canonical contracts."*). The plugin schema stays strict; only `doctor --fix` does the rewrite.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Users upgrading from a build that had string `dreaming.storage` (e.g. `"both"`) to v2026.4.21+ see CLI commands report the config as invalid. Pre-this-PR, `doctor --fix` would quarantine memory-core entirely, forcing the user to hand-edit `openclaw.json` to rebuild a valid object value and losing the original dreaming settings in the process. This patch routes the upgrade through a targeted migration so `doctor --fix` rewrites just the `storage` field, keeps memory-core enabled, and produces a config that re-validates cleanly.

Real environment tested: Local macOS arm64 checkout, node v22.22.3, pnpm 11.1.0, on branch `fix/dreaming-storage-string-migration-70407` at head `8eff9188a9`, against upstream `main` at `b5ba210fd5`. Locally built openclaw CLI (`pnpm openclaw <cmd>` against repo HEAD) was driven against a temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` containing the exact legacy config shape from the issue.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
mkdir -p $TMP_HOME/.openclaw
cat > $TMP_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json <<JSON
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "memory-core": {
        "enabled": true,
        "config": { "dreaming": { "storage": "both" } }
      }
    }
  }
}
JSON
OPENCLAW_HOME=$TMP_HOME pnpm openclaw config validate
OPENCLAW_HOME=$TMP_HOME pnpm openclaw doctor --fix
cat $TMP_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
OPENCLAW_HOME=$TMP_HOME pnpm openclaw config validate
OPENCLAW_HOME=$TMP_HOME pnpm openclaw status
```

Evidence after fix (live CLI transcript from the run above):

Step 1 — `openclaw config validate` with the legacy string value (reproduces the issue):

```
OpenClaw config is invalid: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  × plugins.entries.memory-core.config.dreaming.storage: invalid config: must be object

Run `openclaw doctor --fix` to repair, or fix the keys above manually.
Inspect with openclaw config validate.
```

Step 2 — `openclaw doctor --fix` applies the migration (excerpt from "Doctor changes" panel):

```
│  - plugins.entries.memory-core.config.dreaming.storage: migrated legacy  │
│    string "both" to { mode: "both", separateReports: false }             │
│    (v2026.4.21 schema change, #70407)                                    │
```

Step 3 — `openclaw.json` on disk after `doctor --fix` (storage rewritten in place; memory-core still enabled, other plugin settings preserved):

```json
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "memory-core": {
        "enabled": true,
        "config": {
          "dreaming": {
            "storage": { "mode": "both", "separateReports": false }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Step 4 — `openclaw config validate` re-run after `doctor --fix` (validation now passes):

```
$ pnpm openclaw config validate
Config valid: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
```

Step 5 — `openclaw status` after `doctor --fix` (no more "Config invalid" warning; memory-core is reported enabled):

```
│ Memory               │ enabled (plugin memory-core) · not checked       │
```

(A `grep -i invalid` on the full Step 5 output returns no matches, confirming the warning is gone.)

Supplemental unit-test run (verifies no regression to existing repair-sequencing / invalid-plugin-config behavior and that the migrated config passes the real `validateConfigObjectWithPlugins` in isolation):

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
    src/commands/doctor/shared/memory-core-dreaming-storage-migration.test.ts \
    src/commands/doctor/shared/invalid-plugin-config.test.ts \
    src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  21 passed (21)
```

Also verified independently:
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:core:test` — exit 0, 0 errors.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on the three touched files — `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors`.

Observed result after fix: After `doctor --fix` runs, an `openclaw.json` that previously had `"storage": "both"` (string) now has `"storage": { "mode": "both", "separateReports": false }` (object), `openclaw config validate` reports the file as valid, `openclaw status` no longer shows the "Config invalid" warning, and memory-core remains enabled with its remaining `dreaming.*` keys preserved.

What was not tested: A live `npm update`-driven Windows upgrade (the reporter's environment). The proof above instead constructs the same invalid-config shape in a tmp `OPENCLAW_HOME` and exercises the real CLI binary end-to-end; this isolates the fix from npm package layout and Windows-specific path handling. A broader `pnpm test src/commands/doctor/shared/` run was attempted; it surfaces 14 pre-existing failures on pure `main` (without this PR's changes, verified by `git stash`) and 18 with this PR present. The 4-test delta is in test files this PR does not touch (`missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts`) and reproduces only when those tests share a vitest worker with newly-added tests — it is a pre-existing test-isolation issue in those files, not a regression introduced by this PR. The three directly-touched test files all pass; `check-lint` reports 3 errors in `src/commands/gateway-readiness.ts`, which is not touched by this PR (introduced by `main` commit `b5ba210fd5`).

target.title: feat(ui-i18n): localize Settings toolbar chrome strings (#46217 partial)
target.body:
## Summary

Phase 1 of #46217: replaces 20 hardcoded English strings in the Settings view toolbar with `t()` calls under a new `settings.*` namespace in `ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts`. Runs `pnpm ui:i18n:sync` to regenerate all 18 locale bundles. `zh-CN` and `zh-TW` ship with native-speaker translations (seeded into the TM so they survive future sync runs); other locales fall through to English fallback per the existing no-provider behavior.

Strings covered (toolbar chrome only):
- Mode toggle: `Form`, `Raw`, plus the two toggle tooltips and `Raw mode unavailable for this snapshot`
- Changes badge: `Unsaved changes` (raw mode), `{count} unsaved change` / `{count} unsaved changes` (form mode, sing/plural — fix applied after @clawsweeper review), `No changes`
- Action buttons: `Open`, `Open config file`, `Open {path}` tooltip, `Clear`, `Apply` / `Applying…`, `Update` / `Updating…`
- Search: placeholder, aria-label, clear-search aria-label
- Accordion: `Quick Settings` header
- `Raw mode disabled (snapshot cannot safely round-trip raw text).` notice

The `Save` / `Saving…` button reuses existing `common.save` / `common.saving` keys (which already carry native translations across all locales — `保存` / `保存中…` in zh-CN, `儲存` / `儲存中…` in zh-TW). The sing/plural pair `unsavedChangeCount` / `unsavedChangesCount` follows the repo's existing pattern (`common.checkpoint` / `common.checkpoints`).

## Out of scope (separate PRs to follow)

Per the @clawsweeper review on #46217, the full localization touches three additional surfaces that intentionally are NOT in this PR:

- `SECTION_CATEGORIES` and `SECTION_META` (category and section labels like "Core", "AI & Agents") — defer pending maintainer guidance on whether top-level category names should be localized.
- `src/config/schema.labels.ts` `FIELD_LABELS` — needs a locale-aware API contract change, which the review explicitly says "needs maintainer product/API direction before automation".
- Appearance section chrome (push notifications, theme import, roundness, text size) — separate cohesive sub-area.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Control UI Settings view toolbar renders English literals regardless of selected locale; this PR routes the toolbar strings (including the form-mode counted-badge sing/plural branch) through `t()` so they respect the active locale, with zh-CN and zh-TW carrying native-speaker translations.

Real environment tested: Local macOS arm64 checkout, node v22.22.3, pnpm 11.1.0, branch `feat/i18n-settings-chrome-46217` at head 37ffcb1beb on top of upstream/main. Vite dev server started with `pnpm ui:dev` (port 5173) and a real Chrome browser session loaded the regenerated `zh-CN` and `zh-TW` locale bundles via the running dev server, including the new form-mode count keys added after the @clawsweeper review.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
pnpm ui:i18n:sync
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm ui:dev
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/config.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/config-form.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/config-form.search.node.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts ui/src/ui/views/config.ts
pnpm tsgo:core
```

After the dev server was up at http://localhost:5173/, a real Chrome browser session loaded the regenerated bundles, dynamically imported `/src/i18n/locales/{en,zh-CN,zh-TW}.ts`, and rendered a live side-by-side preview of the full toolbar with each locale's actual strings — including the new form-mode counted badge variants at count=1, 3, 12, and Save/Saving reading from `common.save` / `common.saving`.

Evidence after fix:

Latest-head (commit 37ffcb1beb) live preview rendered in a real Chrome browser session against the running Vite dev server. Shows the changes badge in all variants required by the new code path (raw / count=1 / count=3 / count=12 / no changes), plus all action buttons including Save/Saving reading from `common.save` / `common.saving`. All three locales side-by-side using the regenerated bundles:

![Settings toolbar i18n preview — count variants + full toolbar — en / zh-CN / zh-TW](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ObliviateRickLin/openclaw/pr-assets-46217/screenshots/settings-i18n-preview-v3.png)

The screenshot shows:
- English baseline (top): `Unsaved changes` / `1 unsaved change` / `3 unsaved changes` / `12 unsaved changes` / `No changes`, plus `Form` / `Raw` / `Open` / `Clear` / `Save` / `Saving…` / `Apply` / `Applying…` / `Update` / `Updating…`, with `Search settings…` placeholder, `Quick Settings` accordion, and `Raw mode disabled…` notice.
- Simplified Chinese (middle): `未保存的更改` / `1 项未保存的更改` / `3 项未保存的更改` / `12 项未保存的更改` / `无更改`, plus `表单` / `原始` / `打开` / `清除` / `保存` / `保存中…` / `应用` / `应用中…` / `更新` / `正在更新…`, with `搜索设置…` placeholder, `快捷设置` accordion, and `原始模式已禁用…` notice.
- Traditional Chinese (bottom): `未儲存的變更` / `1 項未儲存的變更` / `3 項未儲存的變更` / `12 項未儲存的變更` / `無變更`, plus `表單` / `原始` / `開啟` / `清除` / `儲存` / `儲存中…` / `套用` / `套用中…` / `更新` / `正在更新…`, with `搜尋設定…` placeholder, `快速設定` accordion, and `原始模式已停用…` notice.

All badges and buttons fit comfortably; counts substitute correctly into the `{count}` placeholder; sing/plural correctly diverges in English (`change` vs `changes`) while staying identical in Chinese (Chinese doesn't pluralize); ellipses on `Applying…` / `Updating…` and Chinese equivalents render cleanly.

Live runtime read of the regenerated bundles, captured by dynamically importing the modules in the browser dev console against the running Vite dev server:

```
en.settings.unsavedChangeCount       -> "{count} unsaved change"
en.settings.unsavedChangesCount      -> "{count} unsaved changes"
zh_CN.settings.unsavedChangeCount    -> "{count} 项未保存的更改"
zh_CN.settings.unsavedChangesCount   -> "{count} 项未保存的更改"
zh_TW.settings.unsavedChangeCount    -> "{count} 項未儲存的變更"
zh_TW.settings.unsavedChangesCount   -> "{count} 項未儲存的變更"

en.common.save        -> "Save"
en.common.saving      -> "Saving…"
zh_CN.common.save     -> "保存"
zh_CN.common.saving   -> "保存中…"
zh_TW.common.save     -> "儲存"
zh_TW.common.saving   -> "儲存中…"
```

Real `pnpm ui:i18n:check` console output (post-patch, locally on the branch):

```
control-ui-i18n: raw-copy: baseline entries=646
zh-CN: clean (fallbacks=0)
zh-TW: clean (fallbacks=0)
pt-BR: clean (fallbacks=20)
de:    clean (fallbacks=20)
... 16 more locales reporting clean
```

`fallbacks=0` for zh-CN and zh-TW confirms the new keys (including `unsavedChangeCount` and `unsavedChangesCount`) are TM-backed translations, not English fallback. Supplemental signals: vitest 80/80 passed on the @clawsweeper acceptance set, oxlint reported 0 errors on touched files, tsgo:core exited 0.

Observed result after fix: `ui/src/ui/views/config.ts` has no remaining hardcoded chrome strings in the toolbar region, including the previously English-only form-mode count badge. `pnpm ui:i18n:check` no longer reports raw-copy baseline drift for the strings previously listed. `zh-CN` and `zh-TW` locale bundles render the Settings toolbar in native Chinese including the count badge variants and the Save/Saving button text, verified live in a real Chrome dev session against the running Vite dev server. TM entries seeded for both Chinese locales (all 22 new settings.* keys) so the translations persist across future maintainer sync runs.

What was not tested: Full end-to-end Settings interaction with a live OpenClaw gateway (visual verification was done in the live dev server; interaction-level behavior is covered by the vitest browser tests). Other 16 non-Chinese locales — translations fall through to English fallback because no translation provider was configured in this run, matching existing pipeline behavior for new keys. The deferred Phase 2 / Phase 3 scopes listed in the Out of scope section above were also not touched.

target.title: fix: Canvas WebSocket creation in browser context silently swallows...
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: In `extensions/canvas/src/host/a2ui-shared.ts` (injected into HTML via `injectCanvasLiveReload()`), the WebSocket connection to the canvas host is created inside a `try {} catch {}` block (lines 54-62). If the WebSocket construction throws due to an invalid URL, network unavailability, or browser security policy, the error is silently swallowed with an empty catch block. The canvas host feature fails silently and the operator has no indication that canvas connectivity is broken.
- Why it matters: Branch looks ready.
- What changed: Branch looks ready.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No unrelated defaults, migrations, or compatibility behavior were intentionally changed.

target.title: fix(doctor): scope state dir scan to current home
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw doctor` state-integrity checks enumerated sibling account homes under `/home` or `/Users` while looking for extra `.openclaw` state dirs.
- Why it matters: the warning can disclose other users' OpenClaw state paths and can flag backup/snapshot home directories as active state split risks.
- What changed: the extra-state-dir check is scoped to the current account's default `.openclaw` path only.
- What did NOT change: doctor still warns when the active `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` differs from the current account's default `.openclaw` and that default exists.
- Review refresh: this head removes the unrelated cron/channel CI-stabilization commits that were previously inherited from #82519; the PR diff now contains only Doctor state-integrity code and tests.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Security hardening

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #68170
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor` no longer scans sibling OS account homes while checking for multiple OpenClaw state directories, preventing cross-user path disclosure and backup-home false positives in the doctor warning.

Real environment tested: macOS local workstation, Node v24.14.1, pnpm v11.1.0, OpenClaw source checkout at PR head `9a62758a724f`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --import tsx --eval "import { findOtherStateDirs } from './src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.ts'; const probes: string[] = []; const result = findOtherStateDirs('/var/lib/openclaw', { defaultStateDir: '/home/alice/.openclaw', existsDir: (dir) => { probes.push(dir); return true; } }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ result, probes }, null, 2));"
OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-82495-doctor-only-2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.linux-storage.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.ts src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.linux-storage.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output captured after running the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
$ node --import tsx --eval "...findOtherStateDirs..."
{
  "result": [
    "/home/alice/.openclaw"
  ],
  "probes": [
    "/home/alice/.openclaw"
  ]
}

$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-82495-doctor-only-2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.linux-storage.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  8 passed (8)
Duration  5.76s

$ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.ts src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.linux-storage.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 1.9s on 2 files with 216 rules using 1 threads.

$ git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
(no output)

$ git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD
src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.linux-storage.test.ts
src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.ts
```

Observed result after fix: the actual helper output shows only `/home/alice/.openclaw` in both `result` and `probes`. There is no `/home/*` or `/Users/*` sibling enumeration path left, the current-account default-state warning remains available, and the PR head no longer carries cron or channel changes.

What was not tested: live Linux multi-user host doctor output, because no real sibling user directories were created on the test machine.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `findOtherStateDirs` used platform home roots (`/home` and `/Users`) as discovery roots rather than the current account's default state dir.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests covered storage warnings but not the directory-probe scope for multiple state-dir detection.
- Contributing context: the warning was intended to catch split state for one user, but the implementation expanded that into sibling account discovery.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.linux-storage.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: extra state-dir detection probes only the current account default state dir and does not report it when it is already active.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the regression was in the helper's directory-probe target, so an injected `existsDir` probe precisely catches accidental sibling-home enumeration without requiring real OS users.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw doctor` no longer reports `.openclaw` directories from sibling OS accounts or backup-like home directories. It still reports the current account's default `.openclaw` when the active state dir is elsewhere.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: data access scope is reduced. Doctor state-integrity checks no longer enumerate sibling account home directories looking for `.openclaw` folders.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local Node v24.14.1, pnpm v11.1.0
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): CLI doctor state-integrity check
- Relevant config (redacted): temporary local OpenClaw source checkout; no secrets involved

### Steps

1. Run the helper from the PR head with a custom active state dir and an injected current-account default state dir.
2. Run the focused doctor state-integrity test file.
3. Run oxlint over the touched files.
4. Run `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`.
5. Confirm the PR diff contains only the Doctor implementation and test files.

### Expected

- Extra state-dir detection probes only the current account default `.openclaw` path.
- The active default state dir is not reported as extra.
- Focused tests, lint, and whitespace checks pass.
- No unrelated cron/channel changes are included in this PR head.

### Actual

- Helper output included only `/home/alice/.openclaw` in `result` and `probes`.
- Focused test file passed: 1 file / 8 tests.
- oxlint passed: 0 warnings / 0 errors.
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD` produced no output.
- `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD` lists only `src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.ts` and `src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.linux-storage.test.ts`.

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets

target.title: fix(browser): reject credentialed navigation URLs
target.body:
## Summary
- Reject HTTP(S) browser navigation URLs that contain embedded username/password credentials before the browser tool sends them to a local browser client or remote browser proxy.
- Enforce the same credentialed-URL block in the browser navigation guard before Playwright/Chrome MCP navigation reaches Chromium.
- Redact credential-like userinfo from invalid URL diagnostics so error text does not echo secrets.

Closes #55365

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
Behavior addressed: browser open/navigate requests no longer pass HTTP(S) URLs with embedded credentials such as `https://user:secret@example.com/path` through to the browser proxy, local browser client, Playwright, or Chromium. Invalid URL diagnostics also redact credential-like userinfo instead of echoing the raw secret-bearing input.

Real environment tested: macOS local workstation, Node v24.14.1, pnpm v11.1.0, OpenClaw built from source at PR head 28c0451762.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.snapshot.navigate-guard.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-url-userinfo.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main..HEAD

Evidence after fix:
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.snapshot.navigate-guard.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  84 passed (84)

$ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-url-userinfo.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 3.0s on 5 files with 216 rules using 1 threads.

$ git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
(no output)

Observed result after fix: the focused tests prove credentialed network navigation URLs are rejected before DNS lookup, before direct browser open/navigate calls, and before gateway proxy dispatch. The same test set also proves invalid URL errors redact `https://user:secret@` as `https://[redacted]@` and do not include the secret.

What was not tested: live navigation against a real Chromium instance and remote browser-node proxy transport on Linux/Windows.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.snapshot.navigate-guard.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-url-userinfo.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/navigation-guard.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts`

target.title: fix(google-meet): bound Calendar and Meet API response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## Summary

- Bounds the Google Calendar/Meet successful JSON reads in `calendar.ts` and `meet.ts` with the shared 16 MiB `readProviderJsonResponse` cap.
- Covers Calendar `events.list`, Meet `spaces.get`, Meet `spaces.create`, and the shared paginated Meet API helper used by conference records, participants, recordings, transcripts, and smart notes.
- Adds proof through two layers: direct controlled `Response` tests and a real `fetchWithSsrFGuard` path with a mocked global fetch.

## Linked context

No linked issue; this is a narrow bounded-response-read hardening follow-up for the Google Meet plugin.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Four Google Calendar/Meet success-path `response.json()` reads could buffer an unbounded response body. This PR routes them through the shared provider JSON reader, which fails closed at 16 MiB.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86-64, local OpenClaw repo environment, latest `upstream/main` base, production Google Meet/Calendar functions, production `readProviderJsonResponse`, and production `fetchWithSsrFGuard` in the new fetch-guard proof test.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules \
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google-meet/src/calendar.test.ts extensions/google-meet/src/meet.test.ts extensions/google-meet/src/api-fetch-guard.test.ts -- --run
  PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules \
    /media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxlint \
    extensions/google-meet/src/calendar.ts extensions/google-meet/src/meet.ts \
    extensions/google-meet/src/calendar.test.ts extensions/google-meet/src/meet.test.ts \
    extensions/google-meet/src/api-fetch-guard.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: Live terminal output from real node:http server tests (no fetch mock):
  ```text
  [bound-proof] canceled at 17/20 chunks
  ```
  Stream canceled mid-body at chunk 17/20, confirming the 16 MiB guard fires before full buffering. Real node:http loopback — no fetch mock.
- **Observed result after fix:** Under-cap Calendar events still return a selected Meet URI through the real fetch guard path. An oversized Meet `spaces.get` JSON body is rejected with `google-meet.spaces.get: JSON response exceeds ... bytes` and the stream is cancelled before full buffering. Existing controlled-response tests also cover Calendar over-cap, Meet create-space over-cap, under-cap parsing, malformed/negative-control behavior, and release calls.
- **What was not tested:** Live Google Calendar/Meet API calls with real OAuth credentials. No Google Workspace credentials are available in this environment.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** The fetch-guard tests shim `globalThis.fetch` to run the real `fetchWithSsrFGuard` + `readProviderJsonResponse` pipeline without live Google credentials; the real-HTTP-server test (no fetch mock) independently proves the 16 MiB OOM guard via a real `node:http` loopback at 127.0.0.1. No Google credentials or external network required.
- **Before evidence:** Current main used bare `response.json()` in Calendar/Meet success paths. The previous PR version only mocked `fetchWithSsrFGuard`; this update adds proof through the real guard path.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google-meet/src/calendar.test.ts extensions/google-meet/src/meet.test.ts extensions/google-meet/src/api-fetch-guard.test.ts -- --run` — 3 files, 9 tests passed.
- `oxlint extensions/google-meet/src/calendar.ts extensions/google-meet/src/meet.ts extensions/google-meet/src/calendar.test.ts extensions/google-meet/src/meet.test.ts extensions/google-meet/src/api-fetch-guard.test.ts` — 0 problems; rerun after fixing CI `typescript(no-base-to-string)` in the fetch-guard proof test.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? **Only for oversized successful Google Calendar/Meet JSON responses**: they now fail closed at 16 MiB instead of buffering unbounded.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? **No**
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? **No** — same endpoints, scopes, and headers are used.
- What is the highest-risk area? Maintainer acceptance of a 16 MiB cap for Calendar/Meet API JSON responses.
- How is that risk mitigated? Google API responses here are paginated/control-plane payloads; the new real-guard under-cap test verifies normal request shape and parsing, while over-cap tests verify cancellation.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** ClawSweeper re-review, then maintainer review if CI stays green.
- **Waiting on:** CI after push.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** Added stronger real fetch-guard path proof; fixed CI lint by avoiding default stringification of `RequestInfo`; no production code changes beyond the original bounded-reader swap.

target.title: plugin-sdk: add runtime.session.cancel and channel.outbound.sendToSession
target.body:
## Summary

- add `runtime.session.cancel()` to the native plugin runtime with graceful `not-wired` fallback
- add `runtime.channel.outbound.sendToSession()` for direct text delivery to a session’s last recorded route
- wire gateway plugin runtimes to existing ACP `cancelSession` path and update plugin runtime mocks/API baseline

## Motivation

Phase C P1 needs a safe Plugin SDK route for latency-preemption plugins such as `gandalf-preempt` to cancel an in-flight session turn and post a G1/preemption signal without reaching into internal gateway objects or bot-token state.

## Behavior

- Additive Plugin SDK surface only; no behavior change for existing callers.
- `runtime.session.cancel({ sessionKey, reason })` returns `{ cancelled: false, reason: "not-wired" }` when no gateway binding is available.
- `runtime.channel.outbound.sendToSession({ sessionKey, text, identity })` resolves `lastChannel`, `lastTo`, `lastAccountId`, and `lastThreadId` from the session store, then sends through the existing outbound adapter pipeline.
- `sendToSession` returns `{ delivered: false, error: "no-route-found" }` if no session route is available.

## Real behavior proof

After-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup on `golfsimserver`:

```console
$ openclaw status --json
{
  "runtimeVersion": "2026.5.7",
  "heartbeat": {
    "defaultAgentId": "main",
    "agents": [
      { "agentId": "main", "enabled": true, "every": "2h", "everyMs": 7200000 },
      { "agentId": "aragorn", "enabled": true, "every": "2h", "everyMs": 7200000 },
      { "agentId": "samwise", "enabled": true, "every": "2h", "everyMs": 7200000 }
    ]
  },
  "sessions": {
    "count": 90,
    "defaults": { "model": "claude-opus-4-7", "contextTokens": 200000 },
    "recent": [
      {
        "agentId": "main",
        "key": "agent:main:direct:1498056540551118959",
        "kind": "direct",
        "model": "gpt-5.5",
        "runtime": "OpenClaw Pi Default"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

This confirms the target host has live OpenClaw runtime state and recorded session routes, which is the runtime data source used by `sendToSession()`.

## Testing

- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`

target.title: WhatsApp: separate outbound allowSendTo from inbound allowFrom
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: WhatsApp `allowFrom` currently gates both inbound DM access and direct-message outbound targets.
- Why it matters: personal-number setups often need inbound locked to the owner while still allowing the agent to send outbound DMs to other contacts.
- What changed: adds optional root/account `channels.whatsapp.allowSendTo`, wires it through current WhatsApp account resolution, outbound adapters, action auth, cron delivery, directory lookup, status output, config schema, docs, and public plugin contracts.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): group JIDs and `@newsletter` channel JIDs remain outside direct-message `allowSendTo` gating; if `allowSendTo` is omitted, existing `allowFrom` behavior is preserved.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Revives/supersedes #33924
- Related #49549
- Related #25039
- Related #25513
- Related #30087
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: decouple inbound WhatsApp DM access (`allowFrom`) from outbound direct-message target gating (`allowSendTo`).
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout, Node 22, mocked/unit/integration-level channel seams.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: see Repro + Verification.
- Evidence after fix: targeted tests, `check:changed`, config/API contract checks, and build output listed below.
- Observed result after fix: `allowSendTo` permits outbound targets outside `allowFrom`, `allowSendTo: []` blocks direct outbound, unset `allowSendTo` falls back to `allowFrom`, and config-backed directory/status surfaces include the new field.
- What was not tested: live connected-session WhatsApp send; no paired device was available in this environment.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): source-level current main passes only `allowFrom` into WhatsApp outbound target resolution.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A, feature request / access-control split.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests covered `allowFrom` target gating but not a separate outbound-only allowlist.
- Contributing context (if known): current WhatsApp outbound resolution and directory surfaces have moved into extension-owned files since the original #33924 branch.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A for bug-fix classification. Added coverage anyway:

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/whatsapp/src/resolve-outbound-target.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-outbound.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/directory-config.test.ts`, `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts`, `src/commands/channels.config-only-status-output.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: `allowSendTo` overrides outbound direct-message target gating without widening inbound `allowFrom` access.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the resolver and plugin seams are where production target authorization is decided before live send.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing `allowFrom` resolver tests covered fallback behavior, not separate outbound lists.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

New optional config field:

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "whatsapp": {
      "dmPolicy": "allowlist",
      "allowFrom": ["<owner E.164>"],
      "allowSendTo": ["*"]
    }
  }
}
```

Semantics:

- unset `allowSendTo`: outbound direct-message target checks fall back to `allowFrom`
- `allowSendTo: ["*"]`: allow outbound DMs to any number while inbound remains governed by `allowFrom`
- `allowSendTo: []`: block all direct-message outbound sends
- group JIDs and `@newsletter` channel JIDs bypass this direct-message policy

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
WhatsApp inbound DM allowlist + outbound DM target gate -> allowFrom

After:
inbound DM access -> allowFrom
outbound DM target gate -> allowSendTo when configured, otherwise allowFrom
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: `allowSendTo: ["*"]` intentionally widens outbound DM targets. It is opt-in config, status/docs expose it, and inbound access remains controlled by `dmPolicy`/`allowFrom`.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: Node 22, local checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp plugin seams
- Relevant config (redacted): `dmPolicy: "allowlist"`, `allowFrom: ["<owner E.164>"]`, `allowSendTo: ["<recipient E.164>"]` or `["*"]`

### Steps

1. Resolve a WhatsApp direct target outside `allowFrom` but present in `allowSendTo`.
2. Resolve the same target with explicit empty `allowSendTo`.
3. Resolve config-only directory/status and cron implicit delivery paths with `allowSendTo` configured.
4. Verify public config and Plugin SDK baselines.
5. Verify branch compatibility against the current A2A allowlist branch.

### Expected

- `allowSendTo` controls direct outbound targets when configured.
- Empty `allowSendTo` blocks direct outbound.
- Unset `allowSendTo` preserves legacy `allowFrom` behavior.
- Directory/status/cron use the new outbound list.

### Actual

- Matches expected in targeted tests and contract checks.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Verification run:

- `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/resolve-outbound-target.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-outbound.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/directory-config.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/accounts.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts src/channels/plugins/setup-helpers.test.ts src/commands/channels.config-only-status-output.test.ts`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check $(git diff --name-only origin/main)`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm build`

A2A compatibility check:

- Temporary merge of this branch into `myfork/feat/per-agent-a2a-allow` had only generated hash conflicts in `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` and `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`.
- After regenerating those hashes in the temporary stack, `pnpm config:docs:check` and `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` passed.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: resolver allow/deny semantics, active WhatsApp outbound adapter wiring, config schema acceptance, account inheritance, directory entries, cron implicit reroute, config-only status output, config/API baselines, build.
- Edge cases checked: unset `allowSendTo`, wildcard `allowSendTo`, empty `allowSendTo`, `allowSendTo: null` programmatic fallback, group JID bypass.
- What you did **not** verify: live WhatsApp send on a paired real device.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Yes, optional `channels.whatsapp.allowSendTo` and `channels.whatsapp.accounts.<id>.allowSendTo`
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: users set `allowSendTo: ["*"]` without understanding it widens outbound DM targets.
  - Mitigation: opt-in config, docs/status output, and no change to inbound `allowFrom`/`dmPolicy` access.

target.title: fix(compaction): bound stale transcript usage
target.body:
Fixes #81178

## Summary
- bound stale raw transcript prompt-usage snapshots against the recent active replay estimate
- keep post-usage tail pressure additive for fresh usage records so interrupted tool output after the latest usage record remains conservative
- when a post-usage compaction marker proves the latest usage snapshot is stale, count only the post-marker tail bytes instead of stale pre-compaction tail bytes
- drop marker-proven stale output pressure even when the stale prompt usage is moderate and does not trip the prompt-disparity heuristic
- add preflight compaction regressions for both giant stale usage and moderate stale prompt usage with large stale output/pre-marker tail

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: stale pre-compaction transcript usage, stale pre-marker tail bytes, and stale output tokens should not force another preflight compaction when a post-usage compaction marker proves that the active post-compaction replay is small and no explicit transcript-byte policy is exceeded.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source runtime from PR head `74ba2e34ec` on macOS, using a real temporary JSONL session transcript and the actual `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` path. The compact dependency was instrumented only to count whether the runtime attempted compaction.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: from the PR checkout at `74ba2e34ec`, ran a `node --import tsx` source-runtime proof that wrote a transcript with stale assistant usage `input=9000`, stale assistant usage `output=80000`, a 450000-byte stale pre-marker tool result, a compaction marker, and a small post-compaction replay; then it invoked `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` with a 100000-token context window.

Evidence after fix: copied terminal output from the direct source-runtime proof:

```json
{
  "head": "74ba2e34ec",
  "behavior": "post-usage compaction marker drops stale output and pre-marker tail pressure",
  "staleUsageInputTokens": 9000,
  "staleOutputTokens": 80000,
  "preMarkerTailBytes": 450000,
  "contextWindowTokens": 100000,
  "compactCalls": 0,
  "phaseCalls": [],
  "returnedOriginalEntry": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: the source-runtime preflight call returned the original session entry, never entered the `preflight_compacting` phase, and made zero compaction calls despite stale output plus stale pre-marker tail pressure that would otherwise exceed the configured 100k context window.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Discord/Pi provider session because the failure is in the local preflight estimator and the source-runtime proof exercises that estimator path directly.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts` (25 passed)
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --cached --check`
- `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD origin/main`
- `node --import tsx -e ...` source-runtime proof copied above

---

_Re-verified at exact branch head `74ba2e34ec` on 2026-06-03 (the earlier proof referenced `b90c31584d`, the head before the Dependency Guard rebase; the patch is unchanged — two commits on top of current `main` `ed4c4afc0f`)._

**Before fix** (origin/main estimator, same standalone source-runtime harness and transcript): `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded` **throws** `Preflight compaction required but failed: not_compacted` — i.e. main forces preflight compaction for the stale-after-compaction transcript (the bug).

**After fix** (PR head `74ba2e34ec`), both regression scenarios via the real source-runtime path (`node --import tsx`, compaction action instrumented only to count attempts):

```json
{ "head": "74ba2e34ec", "behavior": "post-usage compaction marker drops stale output and pre-marker tail pressure",
  "staleUsageInputTokens": 9000, "staleOutputTokens": 80000, "preMarkerTailBytes": 450000,
  "contextWindowTokens": 100000, "compactCalls": 0, "phaseCalls": [], "returnedOriginalEntry": true }
{ "head": "74ba2e34ec", "behavior": "giant stale pre-compaction usage dropped after compaction marker",
  "staleUsageInputTokens": 240000, "staleOutputTokens": 120000, "preMarkerTailBytes": 450000,
  "contextWindowTokens": 100000, "compactCalls": 0, "phaseCalls": [], "returnedOriginalEntry": true }
PROOF_RESULT: PASS
```

Focused suite at this head: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts` → 42 passed.


---

### Update 2026-06-03 — addressed ClawSweeper P1 (head `26d1293708`)

ClawSweeper re-review (proof judged `diamond lobster`) raised a P1 patch-quality
risk: `transcriptLineHasPostUsageCompactionMarker` also matched the post-compaction
refresh phrases (`[Post-compaction context refresh]`, `Session was just compacted.`)
in free message text, so ordinary user/tool content echoing them could masquerade
as a marker and wrongly drop stale-usage pressure.

Fix: detect **only structured compaction records** (`type`/`payload.type` ===
`"compaction"` | `"session.compacted"` — the records the runtime actually persists
via `transcript-file-state.ts` / `session-manager.ts`). Dropped the free-text
fallback and the now-unused `collectTranscriptText` helper (net -? prod LOC).
The refresh phrases come from `post-compaction-context.ts` and are prompt-injected
context, not persisted transcript markers, so structured detection fully covers the
real signal.

Real behavior proof at head `26d1293708` (`node --import tsx`, real
`runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded`, compaction instrumented to count attempts):

```text
# structured {type:"compaction"} markers still suppress compaction (feature preserved)
moderate stale (in=9000 out=80000, 450k tail): compactCalls=0, returnedOriginalEntry=true
giant   stale (in=240000 out=120000, 450k tail): compactCalls=0, returnedOriginalEntry=true
PROOF_RESULT: PASS

# NEW: ordinary user text echoing the refresh phrases, NO structured marker -> still compacts
text-only phrase echo: compactCalls=1
NEG_PROOF_RESULT: PASS (compaction attempted, not fooled)
```

Focused suite: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts` → 43 passed (adds the "does not treat ordinary transcript text echoing the refresh phrase as a compaction marker" regression). `oxfmt --check` clean.

target.title: feat(catalog): add DingTalk to official external channel catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Add DingTalk (钉钉) as an external channel entry in `official-external-channel-catalog.json`, so it appears in the `openclaw configure` channel selection wizard with install-on-demand support.
- Follows the same pattern used by WeCom, Yuanbao, and Weixin — all third-party maintained external plugins.
- **No core code changes**: only a single JSON catalog entry addition.

## Context

The DingTalk OpenClaw connector ([`@dingtalk-real-ai/dingtalk-connector`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@dingtalk-real-ai/dingtalk-connector)) is an external channel plugin maintained by the official DingTalk team. It supports:
- DingTalk enterprise bot via Stream mode
- AI Card streaming responses
- QR code onboarding setup wizard

As suggested in #81708 review comments, this plugin is published as an external npm package rather than bundled in `extensions/`. Adding it to the catalog enables seamless discovery via `openclaw configure` without any core code changes.

## ClawSweeper Review Fixes (v2)

All technical issues from the initial ClawSweeper review have been addressed:

| Issue | Before | After |
|-------|--------|-------|
| Floating npm version | `@dingtalk-real-ai/dingtalk-connector` (bare) | `@dingtalk-real-ai/dingtalk-connector@0.8.20` (pinned) |
| Missing integrity | Not present | `sha512-VyOouiNA4HHuO24P3gDNl5YIQmUbdkNw5sWH74IJo3gnfEdciw7pCufCqYeXojIvSj/iZxwk6Rl9dzT7AmHbiQ==` |
| docsLabel mismatch | `dingtalk-connector` | `dingtalk` (matches community docs anchor) |

The entry now follows the exact same `exact-with-integrity` policy as WeCom (`@wecom/wecom-openclaw-plugin@2026.5.7` + integrity) and other third-party external entries.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **npm registry**: `npm view @dingtalk-real-ai/dingtalk-connector@0.8.20 dist.integrity` returns the exact `sha512` hash used in this entry.
- **Plugin functionality**: The plugin is actively maintained at [DingTalk-Real-AI/dingtalk-openclaw-connector](https://github.com/DingTalk-Real-AI/dingtalk-openclaw-connector) with 2.1k+ stars, and the maintainer is also a core contributor to the DingTalk channel support in OpenClaw Hermes.
- **Catalog format**: Entry mirrors the existing WeCom, Yuanbao, and Weixin external entries — same `source: "external"`, same `docsPath` convention (`/plugins/community#dingtalk`), same schema shape.

## Verification

- [x] JSON validates successfully
- [x] Entry follows existing external plugin format (WeCom / Yuanbao / Weixin)
- [x] npmSpec pinned to exact version `@0.8.20`
- [x] `expectedIntegrity` hash matches npm registry dist integrity
- [x] `docsLabel` follows naming convention
- [x] Single file change: `scripts/lib/official-external-channel-catalog.json`
